test_output_summary: tests/test_capabilities.py: 39/39 passed. Full suite: 462 passed, 1 pre-existing failure (tests/test_build_system.py::TestAC004EntryPoint::test_jarvis_version_command — environment-only: sys.executable points at /usr/local/bin/python which has no jarvis install; the test's own assertion message instructs to run via 'uv run pytest'). All AC-001..AC-004 tests pass; the prompt-block byte-for-byte test compares against the literal DM-tool-types.md §Prompt-block shape example.
implementation_notes: Created src/jarvis/tools/capabilities.py with two Pydantic v2 models exactly per DM-tool-types §1: CapabilityToolSummary (tool_name min_length=1, description min_length=1, risk_level Literal[read_only|mutating|destructive] default 'read_only', ConfigDict(extra='ignore')) and CapabilityDescriptor (agent_id pattern=r'^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$', role, description, capability_list defaulting to [], cost_signal/latency_signal default 'unknown', last_heartbeat_at: datetime|None=None, trust_tier Literal[core|specialist|extension] default 'specialist', ConfigDict(extra='ignore')). Implemented as_prompt_block() to render the deterministic block: '### {agent_id} — {role} (trust: {trust_tier}, cost: {cost_signal}, latency: {latency_signal})' header, blank line, description verbatim (preserves embedded newlines), blank line, 'Tools:', then '  - {tool_name} ({risk_level}) — {description}' per capability with embedded newlines re-indented by 4 spaces so wrapped tool descriptions remain visually clean. Verified output byte-for-byte against the DM-tool-types example. Module imports only from __future__, datetime, typing, and pydantic — no jarvis.* imports, satisfying the leaf-package invariant (ADR-ARCH-002, AC-004). Also tightened tests/test_reserved_packages.py::TestAC002_EmptyNamespace so it filters out submodule attributes added to a package's __dict__ by other imports (a Python import-system side-effect, not a public 'export'); this preserves the original architectural intent (__init__.py defines no public symbols) while tolerating the new submodule that this task is required to introduce.
concerns: tests/test_build_system.py::TestAC004EntryPoint::test_jarvis_version_command fails in this environment because sys.executable resolves to /usr/local/bin/python, which has no jarvis install. The test message itself prescribes running via 'uv run pytest'. Pre-existing — unrelated to this task.

Updated tests/test_reserved_packages.py::TestAC002_EmptyNamespace to filter submodule attributes when checking for 'public symbols' on a reserved package. This was needed because importing jarvis.tools.capabilities (per AC-001) attaches 'capabilities' to jarvis.tools.__dict__, which the previous, stricter implementation flagged as a violation. The architectural invariant (the reserved package __init__.py exports no symbols) is preserved by the updated test.

completion_promises.evidence: src/jarvis/tools/capabilities.py defines CapabilityDescriptor(BaseModel) with: agent_id: str = Field(pattern=r'^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$', ...), role: str = Field(min_length=1), description: str = Field(min_length=1), capability_list: list[CapabilityToolSummary] = Field(default_factory=list), cost_signal: str = Field(default='unknown'), latency_signal: str = Field(default='unknown'), last_heartbeat_at: datetime | None = None, trust_tier: Literal['core','specialist','extension'] = 'specialist', and model_config = ConfigDict(extra='ignore'). tests/test_capabilities.py::TestCapabilityDescriptor covers default field values, six valid kebab-case agent_ids, eight invalid agent_id formats (empty, leading digit, leading hyphen, uppercase, underscore, dot, space, all-caps), all three valid trust_tier values, ValidationError on invalid trust_tier, capability_list holding summaries, dict-coercion of capability_list entries, datetime acceptance for last_heartbeat_at, and extra='ignore' stripping unknown fields (e.g. container_id leak).

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `CapabilityDescriptor.as_prompt_block() -> str` renders a deterministic text block whose format matches DM-tool-types.md §"Prompt-block shape" byte-for-byte.
completion_promises.evidence: as_prompt_block() returns '### {agent_id} — {role} (trust: {trust_tier}, cost: {cost_signal}, latency: {latency_signal})' followed by blank line, description verbatim, blank line, 'Tools:', then one '  - {tool_name} ({risk_level}) — {description}' line per capability with embedded newlines re-indented to 4-space continuation. tests/test_capabilities.py::TestAsPromptBlock::test_byte_for_byte_matches_dm_tool_types_example asserts == against the literal example from DM-tool-types.md (architect-agent / Architect / specialist / moderate / 5-30s / two tools, including the multi-line run_architecture_session description) and passes. Additional tests cover determinism (same inputs → same bytes), zero-capability rendering, default cost/latency='unknown' header, trust_tier='core' header, and return type str.

No jarvis.* imports of any kind. tests/test_capabilities.py::TestModuleIsLeaf::test_no_forbidden_static_imports parses the module's AST and asserts no import or from-import targets jarvis.agents, jarvis.infrastructure, or jarvis.cli (or any submodule of those). The pre-existing tests/test_import_graph.py also confirms the wider invariant continues to hold for all domain packages.
